Preconditions: Student account exists, approval_status='approved', is_active=true
Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Select 'Student' role
3. Enter email: student@graduate.utm.my
4. Enter password
5. Click Log In button
Expected_Results:
1. Credentials accepted without errors
2. JWT token issued and stored in localStorage and cookie (SameSite=Strict)
3. User redirected to /dashboard/student
4. Zustand authStore reflects student role

Preconditions: Lecturer account exists, approval_status='approved', email_verified=true, assigned to course
Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Select 'Lecturer' role
3. Enter lecturer@utm.my and password
4. Click Log In
Expected_Results:
1. Login succeeds, valid JWT returned
2. User redirected to /dashboard/lecturer
3. Sidebar displays: My Courses, Roster Upload, Smart Grid, Assessment Setup, Queries, Messages, Profile

Preconditions: Coordinator account exists (lecturer with special role), approved and active
Steps:
1. Select 'Lecturer' on login page
2. Enter coordinator@utm.my and password
3. Click Log In
Expected_Results:
1. Login succeeds
2. User redirected to /dashboard/coordinator
3. Navigation visible: Courses, Roster Management, Assessment Config, Semester Timeline, Messages, Flagged Marks, Reports

Preconditions: HOD account exists with role='hod', approved and active
Steps:
1. Enter hod@utm.my and password
2. Click Log In
Expected_Results:
1. User redirected to /dashboard/hod
2. HOD navigation visible: Departments, Analytics, Export, Audit Log

Preconditions: Admin account exists: admin@utm.my, active and approved
Steps: 1. Enter admin credentials and click Log In
Expected_Results:
1. User redirected to /dashboard/admin
2. Admin navigation visible: Pending Approvals, Users, Roles & Permissions, Database, Settings, System Logs

Preconditions: Email not registered OR password incorrect
Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Enter unregistered email or wrong password
3. Click Log In
Expected_Results:
1. Error message displayed: 'Invalid email or password'
2. No JWT token issued
3. User remains on login page

Preconditions: User currently logged in (any role)
Steps:
1. Click user avatar at top-right
2. Select 'Log Out' from dropdown
Expected_Results:
1. POST /auth/logout called
2. localStorage token and cookie cleared
3. Zustand authStore reset
4. User redirected to public landing page (/)

Preconditions: Student email domain is @graduate.utm.my, not already registered
Steps:
1. Navigate to Signup page
2. Select 'Student' role
3. Enter email, full name, matric number, password
4. Click Sign Up
Expected_Results:
1. Account created: role='student', approval_status='pending'
2. Audit log entry created
3. User redirected to pending-approval page

Preconditions: Lecturer uses @utm.my email
Steps:
1. Select 'Lecturer' on signup
2. Enter @utm.my email and valid details
3. Submit form
Expected_Results:
1. Registration succeeds, account created with approval_status='pending'
2. Audit log records signup

Preconditions: User attempts to register as admin/coordinator/hod
Steps:
1. Send POST /auth/signup with role='admin' (or 'coordinator', 'hod')
2. Or inspect frontend role selector
Expected_Results:
1. API returns HTTP 400: 'Only student and lecturer roles can self-register. Admin, coordinator, and HOD accounts are provisioned by administrator.'
2. No user record created

Preconditions: User just signed up, SMTP/OTP service operational
Steps:
1. Complete signup form
2. Check email for OTP code
3. Enter OTP in verification screen
4. Submit
Expected_Results:
1. OTP email delivered in reasonable time
2. Correct OTP sets email_verified=true
3. Incorrect OTP shows validation error (no code revelation)

Preconditions: Lecturer signup exists with approval_status='pending'
Steps: admin logged in
Expected_Results:
1. Navigate to Pending Approvals page
2. Locate pending lecturer entry
3. Click 'Approve'
4. Confirm in dialog
Postconditions:
1. approval_status changes to 'approved'
2. approved_by set to admin's user ID
3. approved_at timestamp recorded
4. Audit log captures approval action

Preconditions: Pending user exists
Steps: admin logged in
Expected_Results:
1. Navigate to Pending Approvals
2. Click 'Reject' on pending user
3. Enter rejection reason
4. Confirm
Postconditions:
1. approval_status changes to 'rejected'
2. rejection_reason stored
3. User cannot log in (appropriate message shown)

Preconditions: Coordinator logged in; course code not existing for same year/semester
Steps:
1. Navigate to Course Management > Create Course
2. Enter: KKK212, name, 3 credits, semester, academic year
3. Leave Lecturer field empty
4. Click Create Course
Expected_Results:
1. Course created with lecturer_id=null
2. No silent auto-assignment occurs
3. Course appears in unassigned list
4. Audit log records creation with old_values=null

Preconditions: Course exists; user has edit permission
Steps:
1. Open course edit form
2. Change name and max_students
3. Click Save Changes
Expected_Results:
1. Course record updated in Supabase
2. Audit log stores old and new values
3. Success toast displayed

Preconditions: Course has enrollments or assessments
Steps:
1. Attempt to delete course
2. Observe system response
3. Use 'Archive' action instead
Expected_Results:
1. Hard delete blocked: 'Cannot delete: academic records exist.'
2. Archive succeeds; course hidden but retained in DB
3. Audit log records archive action

Preconditions: Course has zero enrollments and zero assessments
Steps:
1. Click Delete on course card
2. Confirm deletion in modal
Expected_Results:
1. Course removed from database
2. Audit log records deletion with old_values preserved

Preconditions: Course exists with no lecturer; target lecturer exists and has capacity
Steps:
1. Open Course Management
2. Click 'Assign Lecturer' on unassigned course
3. Select lecturer from dropdown
4. Confirm assignment
Expected_Results:
1. Course.lecturer_id updated
2. Lecturer's workload bar reflects new total
3. Audit log records assignment

Preconditions: Lecturer assigned courses totaling 9 credits in semester
Steps:
1. Attempt to assign additional 3-credit course to same lecturer
2. Observe system response
Expected_Results:
1. Assignment blocked: 'Lecturer exceeds 9-credit limit for this semester.'
2. No database update occurs

Preconditions: HOD logged in; lecturer at or above 9 credits
Steps:
1. HOD navigates to lecturer workload view
2. Selects overloaded lecturer
3. Clicks 'Override Cap' with justification
4. Assigns additional course
Expected_Results:
1. Assignment succeeds despite exceeding 9 credits
2. Audit log records override with reason and HOD ID
3. Workload bar shows excess with red warning indicator

Preconditions: Coordinator on Course Management page; intake year selected
Steps:
1. Click 'Add from Curriculum Library'
2. Select Program: Bachelor of Chemical Process Engineering
3. Select Year 1, Semester 1 courses
4. Click 'Bulk Add to System'
Expected_Results:
1. All selected courses inserted with derived academic_year
2. Courses appear with correct metadata (credits, code, name, semester)
3. No duplicate codes; existing ones skipped or warned

Preconditions: Coordinator logged in; Excel file with email/full_name/matric_number; active semester timeline exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Roster Management
2. Select target course
3. Click 'Upload Roster', choose Excel file
4. Preview parsed roster
5. Click 'Confirm Import'
Expected_Results:
1. Each new student email triggers account provisioning
2. Invitation tokens generated cryptographically
3. New accounts created: is_active=false, invitation_status='pending'
4. Enrollment records created with semester/academic_year from timeline
5. Audit log records upload

Test_Case_Title: Invitation Token Generation for New Students
Preconditions: Roster upload just processed with new emails
Steps:
1. Inspect users table in Supabase
2. Check invitation_token and invitation_status fields
3. Verify token expiration (should be 14 days)
Expected_Results:
1. Each student has unique, cryptographically secure token
2. Token expiration exactly 14 days after creation
3. Password hash derived from token (reset on first login)
4. No default/weak preset password used

Preconditions: Student received invitation email; token not expired
Steps:
1. Student clicks invitation link
2. Enters new strong password
3. Confirms password
4. Submits form
Expected_Results:
1. is_active becomes true
2. invitation_status changes to 'accepted'
3. Password hash re-computed
4. Student can log in with new password

Preconditions: Student account exists and is active; coordinator on roster page
Steps:
1. Click 'Add Student'
2. Search for student by email or matric
3. Select student
4. Click 'Enroll'
Expected_Results:
1. Enrollment record created (student_id + course_id)
2. Status set to 'enrolled'
3. Student appears in roster grid

Preconditions: Student enrolled in course; no published marks exist
Steps:
1. Locate student in roster
2. Click 'Drop'
3. Confirm action
Expected_Results:
1. Enrollment status changes to 'dropped' (or deleted per policy)
2. Student no longer appears in active roster
3. Audit log records drop action

Preconditions: Course exists; lecturer assigned; user has permission
Steps:
1. Navigate to Assessment Setup
2. Select course
3. Click 'Add Assessment'
4. Enter: 'Midterm Exam', type='exam', max_score=100, weight=30%
5. Click Save
Expected_Results:
1. Assessment created and linked to course
2. is_locked defaults to false
3. Assessment appears in list with correct weight

Preconditions: Assessment exists with zero mark records
Steps:
1. Open assessment edit form
2. Change max_score to 50
3. Update weight to 25%
4. Save
Expected_Results:
1. Update succeeds
2. Audit log records old and new values

Test_Case_Title: Block Assessment Update When Marks Exist
Preconditions: Assessment has at least one mark record
Steps:
1. Open assessment edit form
2. Attempt to change max_score or weight
3. Save
Expected_Results:
1. System returns HTTP 409 CONFLICT
2. Error: 'Cannot modify assessment: marks already exist. Unpublish or delete marks first.'
3. No database update occurs

Preconditions: Course assessments have cumulative weight ≠ 100% (e.g.
Expected_Results:
1. Click 'Lock Assessment Schema'
2. Observe response
3. Add missing assessments to reach 100%
4. Click Lock again
Postconditions:
1. First attempt fails: 'Total weight must equal 100%. Current: 70%'
2. After adjustment, lock succeeds
3. is_locked becomes true for all course assessments
4. Further destructive edits blocked until explicitly unlocked

Preconditions: Assessment exists with zero marks
Steps:
1. Click Delete on assessment
2. Confirm deletion
Expected_Results:
1. Assessment removed
2. Audit log records deletion

Preconditions: Assessment has one or more mark records
Steps:
1. Click Delete on assessment
2. Confirm deletion
Expected_Results:
1. Deletion blocked: 'Assessment has marks. Remove marks first or archive course.'
2. Assessment remains in database

Preconditions: Lecturer logged in; assessment exists and not locked; student enrolled
Steps:
1. Navigate to Smart Grid or Assessment Setup
2. Select course and assessment
3. Enter raw_score for student (e.g., 75)
4. Click Save
Expected_Results:
1. Mark record created with status='draft'
2. normalised_score auto-calculated
3. is_flagged defaults to false

Preconditions: Assessment max_score=100; lecturer enters raw_score=75
Steps:
1. Enter raw_score=75 for student
2. Save
3. Query marks table
Expected_Results:
1. normalised_score = (75/100)*100 = 75.0
2. If max_score=50 and raw_score=40, normalised_score=80.0

Preconditions: Draft marks exist; grade submission deadline not passed
Steps:
1. Select assessment
2. Click 'Publish Marks'
3. Confirm in modal
Expected_Results:
1. All draft marks change status to 'published'
2. Students can view on 'My Marks' page
3. Audit log records publish with assessment_id

Test_Case_Title: Block Publish Past Grade Submission Deadline
Preconditions: Current date past grade_submission_deadline for active semester
Steps:
1. Attempt to publish marks for assessment
2. Observe response
Expected_Results:
1. Publish blocked: 'Grade submission deadline has passed. Contact coordinator or HOD for extension.'
2. Marks remain in draft status

Preconditions: Marks currently published; coordinator/admin logged in
Steps:
1. Navigate to assessment marks view
2. Click 'Unpublish'
3. Enter reason: 'Data entry error discovered'
4. Confirm
Expected_Results:
1. Marks revert to status='draft'
2. Students cannot see marks
3. Audit log records unpublish with reason and user ID
4. Lecturer attempting same action receives HTTP 403

Preconditions: Mark record exists; user has permission
Steps:
1. Locate mark in Smart Grid
2. Click 'Flag'
3. Enter flag_note: 'Student claims attendance bonus not applied'
4. Save
Expected_Results:
1. is_flagged becomes true
2. flag_note stored
3. Mark appears in Flagged Marks page
4. Optional ai_anomaly_flag set if ML detection enabled

Preconditions: Student enrolled with published marks; assessments locked (100% weight)
Steps:
1. Student logs in and navigates to 'My Marks'
2. Selects course
3. Views grade breakdown
Expected_Results:
1. Weighted total percentage displayed correctly
2. Letter grade shown (A, A-, B+, etc.) per UTM thresholds
3. GPA value displayed (e.g., 3.67 for A-)
4. If total < 50, 'At Risk' warning shown

Preconditions: Student has weighted total < 50 OR multiple flagged marks
Steps:
1. Coordinator navigates to Reports/Flagged Marks
2. Views 'At-Risk Students' list
Expected_Results:
1. At-risk students listed with course, percentage, risk reason
2. Data sortable and filterable
3. Coordinator can click to view detailed breakdown

Test_Case_Title: Student Raises a Query on a Mark
Preconditions: Student logged in; mark is published
Steps:
1. Navigate to 'My Marks'
2. Locate assessment mark
3. Click 'Raise Query'
4. Enter query text: 'I believe my midterm should be 82, not 72'
5. Submit
Expected_Results:
1. course_queries record created linked to mark_id
2. Query status is 'pending'
3. Lecturer receives notification/message

Preconditions: Query exists with resolved_at=null; lecturer assigned to course
Steps:
1. Lecturer navigates to Queries page
2. Opens pending query
3. Reviews mark and evidence
4. Enters response: 'Verified: attendance penalty due to 3 absences'
5. Clicks 'Resolve'
Expected_Results:
1. lecturer_response stored
2. resolved_at timestamped
3. Student sees response on 'My Marks' or Queries view

Preconditions: Query has been responded to by lecturer
Steps:
1. Student views query status
2. Coordinator views query in master list
Expected_Results:
1. Status shows as 'Resolved' with timestamp
2. Resolved queries filterable separately from open
3. Audit log tracks query activity (not necessarily every query, for privacy)

Preconditions: Mark status is 'draft'
Steps:
1. Student navigates to 'My Marks'
2. Attempts to click 'Raise Query' on draft mark
Expected_Results:
1. 'Raise Query' button disabled or hidden
2. If accessed via API: HTTP 400: 'Cannot query unpublished marks.'

Preconditions: Coordinator logged in; no existing timeline for same year+semester
Steps:
1. Navigate to Semester Timeline page
2. Click 'Create Timeline'
3. Enter: academic_year='2025/2026', semester='1'
4. Set dates and deadlines
5. Save
Expected_Results:
1. Timeline inserted into semester_timelines
2. UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicates
3. Visual timeline bar renders with deadlines marked
4. Mini-calendar highlights deadline dates

Preconditions: Semester timeline exists with past grade_submission_deadline
Steps:
1. Lecturer attempts to publish marks
2. System checks deadline via session.py helper
Expected_Results:
1. Publish blocked with clear error message
2. session helper correctly derives active semester

Preconditions: Timeline exists; coordinator has permission
Steps:
1. Open timeline edit form
2. Extend grade_submission_deadline by one week
3. Save
Expected_Results:
1. Timeline record updated
2. Audit log records change with old/new deadline values
3. Lecturers can now publish until new deadline

Preconditions: Timeline from previous academic year; no active enrollments reference it
Steps:
1. Click Delete on past timeline
2. Confirm
Expected_Results:
1. Timeline removed
2. Audit log records deletion

Preconditions: Sender logged in; recipient exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Messages > Compose
2. Select recipient from dropdown
3. Enter subject and body
4. Click Send
Expected_Results:
1. Message record created in messages table
2. is_read defaults to false
3. Recipient sees message in inbox

Preconditions: Existing message between two users; user is participant
Steps:
1. Open existing message
2. Click Reply
3. Type reply text
4. Send
Expected_Results:
1. New message has parent_message_id set to original ID
2. Thread visually grouped in UI
3. Non-participants cannot inject (403 if attempted)

Preconditions: Student logged in; policy restricts students from messaging HOD directly
Steps:
1. Student opens Compose message
2. Attempts to select HOD from recipient list
3. Or crafts direct API request to HOD
Expected_Results:
1. HOD does not appear in student's dropdown
2. API returns HTTP 403: 'You are not authorized to message this recipient. Message lecturer or coordinator instead.'
3. Student can message enrolled course lecturers/coordinators

Preconditions: Rate limiter configured (e.g.
Steps: 60/hour)
Expected_Results: 1. User sends 61 messages within one hour
Postconditions:
1. First 60 messages succeed
2. 61st returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with retry-after header
3. User sees: 'You have reached messaging limit. Wait before sending more.'

Preconditions: Message exists with is_read=false
Steps:
1. Recipient opens message
2. System auto-marks as read (or user clicks 'Mark as Read')
Expected_Results:
1. is_read becomes true
2. read_at timestamped
3. Unread badge count decreases

Preconditions: User has 3 unread messages
Steps:
1. Observe bell icon in header (shows '3')
2. Open one message (mark as read)
3. Observe badge again
Expected_Results:
1. Badge initially shows '3'
2. After reading one, badge updates to '2' without full refresh

Preconditions: Coordinator creates a course
Steps:
1. Create new course
2. Query audit_logs table
Expected_Results:
1. Entry exists: action='CREATE', entity_type='course', entity_id=<course_id>
2. user_id is coordinator's ID
3. new_values JSONB contains full payload
4. ip_address and user_agent captured

Preconditions: Lecturer publishes marks for assessment
Steps:
1. Publish marks
2. Check audit_logs
Expected_Results:
1. action='PUBLISH_MARKS', entity_type='assessment'
2. old_values and new_values show status transition (draft→published)
3. Timestamp exact

Preconditions: User logs in
Steps:
1. Log in as any role
2. Query audit_logs filtered by action='LOGIN'
Expected_Results:
1. Login event recorded: user_id, ip_address, user_agent, timestamp
2. Failed logins may be logged as action='LOGIN_FAILED'

Preconditions: HOD logged in; department has courses/lecturers/students
Steps:
1. Navigate to HOD Dashboard > Analytics
2. Observe charts and KPIs
Expected_Results:
1. Analytics show: total courses, total students, average GPA, lecturer workload
2. Charts responsive and filterable by semester
3. Data matches underlying tables

Preconditions: Course has published marks for all assessments
Steps:
1. Navigate to Reports
2. Select course and semester
3. Click 'Generate Grade Report'
4. Download report
Expected_Results:
1. Report contains: name, matric number, assessment breakdown, weighted total, letter grade, GPA
2. Format structured (CSV/JSON/DOCX available)
3. Only published marks included

Preconditions: User account has valid role
Steps:
1. Log in
2. Observe URL after authentication
Expected_Results:
1. Student → /dashboard/student
2. Lecturer (no special roles) → /dashboard/lecturer
3. Coordinator → /dashboard/coordinator
4. HOD → /dashboard/hod
5. Admin → /dashboard/admin

Preconditions: Student logged in
Steps: 1. Observe left sidebar after login
Expected_Results:
1. Visible: Dashboard, My Courses, My Marks, Queries, Profile
2. Hidden: Courses, Roster, Assessment Config, Reports, Timeline, Admin tools

Preconditions: Lecturer logged in (not coordinator/hod)
Steps: 1. Observe sidebar
Expected_Results:
1. Visible: Dashboard, My Courses, Roster Upload, Smart Grid, Assessment Setup, Queries, Messages, Profile
2. Hidden: Course Management (full), Flagged Marks, Audit Log

Preconditions: Coordinator logged in
Steps: 1. Observe sidebar
Expected_Results: 1. Visible: Dashboard, Courses, Roster Management, Assessment Config, Semester Timeline, Messages, Flagged Marks, Reports, Profile

Preconditions: HOD logged in
Steps: 1. Observe sidebar
Expected_Results: 1. Visible: Dashboard, Departments, Analytics, Export, Audit Log, Profile

Preconditions: Admin logged in
Steps: 1. Observe sidebar
Expected_Results: 1. Visible: Dashboard, Pending Approvals, Users, Roles & Permissions, Database, Settings, System Logs, Profile
